sections.heading: What is Material Design?
sections.content: Material Design is Google's open-source design system for building beautiful, consistent, and accessible user interfaces across Android, iOS, and the web. It provides a comprehensive set of guidelines, components, and theming tools that help developers and designers create apps that feel native to Android while expressing their own brand identity. The Material Components for Android (MDC-Android) library is the official implementation of Material Design in Android, and it is the recommended replacement for the older AppCompat widgets.

sections.heading: Why Use Material Components?
items: Consistent, polished UI that follows Google's design guidelines out of the box
Accessible by default — components are built with accessibility in mind
Themeable — customize colors, typography, and shapes across the entire app from one place
Rich set of components not available in base Android: BottomNavigationView, ChipGroup, MaterialCardView, and more
Material You (Material 3) supports dynamic color — adapts to the user's wallpaper colors on Android 12+
Regular updates from Google aligned with Android platform updates
Replaces older AppCompat widgets with more feature-rich, visually refined equivalents
Supports both Material 2 (com.google.android.material) and Material 3 (Material You) theming

sections.heading: Material Design 2 vs Material Design 3 (Material You)

rows.material_2: 2018 — used in most existing Android apps
rows.material_3: 2021 — recommended for new projects targeting Android 12+
Expanded tonal palette with dynamic color support from wallpaper
Supported on Android 12+ — adapts to user's wallpaper

rows.material_2: Buttons, Cards, TextFields, BottomNav, FAB, etc.
rows.material_3: All M2 components updated with new shapes, colors, and variants
Refined shape scale with more expressive rounded corners

sections.heading: Setting Up Material Components
sections.content: To use Material Components in your Android project, add the Material Components dependency to your app-level build.gradle.kts. Then update your app's theme in res/values/themes.xml to extend a Material theme instead of an AppCompat theme. Once your theme extends a Material theme, all widgets in your layouts automatically receive the Material styling, and you can use the full range of Material Components in your XML.

sections.heading: Material Theme Attributes You Can Customize
items: colorPrimary – The main brand color used for key UI elements like the AppBar and FAB
colorOnPrimary – Color of content drawn on top of colorPrimary (e.g., icons, text on AppBar)
colorSecondary – Accent color for interactive elements like checkboxes and switches
colorSurface – Background color of surfaces like cards, sheets, and menus
colorOnSurface – Color of content drawn on top of colorSurface
colorError – Color used for error states in TextFields, snackbars, and form validation
textAppearance* – Typography styles (headline, body, label, display) applied globally
shapeAppearance* – Corner radius and shape style (rounded, cut) applied to small/medium/large components

rows.description: Styled button with ripple, elevation, and icon support
Card container with elevation, corner radius, and stroke support
Floating label text field with error, helper text, and icon support
Compact interactive elements for tags, filters, and multi-select
Bottom navigation bar with icons and labels for top-level destinations
Side navigation drawer with menu items and header
Prominent circular action button with icon and optional label
FAB with text label that can expand and collapse
Brief message shown at the bottom of the screen with optional action
rows.xml_tag: Created in code: Snackbar.make(view, message, duration)

rows.description: Material-styled dialog with title, message, and action buttons
Sheet that slides up from the bottom of the screen
Horizontal tab strip for switching between views or Fragments
Top app bar with title, navigation icon, and action menu items
Toolbar that collapses into a smaller toolbar as the user scrolls
Vertical navigation rail for medium-sized screens (tablets, foldables)

sections.heading: MaterialButton
sections.content: MaterialButton is the Material Components replacement for the standard Android Button. It offers built-in support for icons (start, end, or icon-only), ripple effects, stroke borders, corner radius customization, and elevation. MaterialButton comes in several styles out of the box: filled (default), outlined, text (flat), elevated, and tonal — all controlled by the style attribute.

sections.heading: MaterialButton Styles & Attributes
items: style="@style/Widget.Material3.Button" – Filled button (default) with solid background
style="@style/Widget.Material3.Button.OutlinedButton" – Outlined button with border and transparent background
style="@style/Widget.Material3.Button.TextButton" – Flat text-only button with no background or border
style="@style/Widget.Material3.Button.ElevatedButton" – Button with elevation shadow
style="@style/Widget.Material3.Button.TonalButton" – Filled tonal button using secondary container color
app:icon – Drawable resource for an icon displayed inside the button
app:iconGravity – Position of the icon: start, end, top, textStart, textEnd
app:strokeColor and app:strokeWidth – Border color and thickness for outlined style
app:cornerRadius – Overrides the default corner radius for this specific button

rows.appearance: Filled background with floating label — default and most common
Bordered box with floating label above the border
rows.use_case: When a cleaner, outlined look is preferred

rows.appearance: Compact version of the filled style with reduced height
rows.use_case: Dense forms where vertical space is limited

sections.heading: TextInputLayout Key Features
items: android:hint on the inner TextInputEditText becomes the floating label when the field is focused
app:helperText – Persistent helper text shown below the field to guide the user
app:errorText (or setError() in code) – Shows red error message below the field
app:counterEnabled and app:counterMaxLength – Shows a character counter below the field
app:startIconDrawable – Icon placed at the start (left) of the input field
app:endIconMode – Controls the end icon: password_toggle, clear_text, custom, or none
app:boxStrokeColor – Overrides the border/underline color (supports ColorStateList for focused state)
Always wrap TextInputEditText inside TextInputLayout — never use EditText directly with TextInputLayout

rows.use_case: Triggers an action (e.g., Add to calendar, Share)
Multi-select filter options (e.g., category filters in a search screen)
Single-select options within a ChipGroup (e.g., size selector)
Represents user-entered input (e.g., email recipients, tags)

sections.heading: BottomNavigationView Setup
items: Add BottomNavigationView to the bottom of your Activity layout, outside any scrollable content
Create a menu XML file in res/menu/ with <item> entries for each tab (max 5 items recommended)
Each menu item needs an android:id, android:title, and android:icon attribute
Set app:menu="@menu/bottom_nav_menu" on the BottomNavigationView
Listen for tab selection: bottomNav.setOnItemSelectedListener { item -> ... }
Pair with Jetpack Navigation Component using NavigationUI.setupWithNavController() for automatic Fragment switching
app:labelVisibilityMode controls label display: labeled, unlabeled, selected, or auto
Use app:itemActiveIndicatorStyle to customize the active tab indicator shape (Material 3)

rows.snackbar: Bar at the bottom of the screen with optional action button
rows.toast: Floating bubble message — no action button

rows.snackbar: Yes — supports a single clickable action (e.g., Undo)
User can swipe to dismiss; also auto-dismisses
rows.toast: Cannot be dismissed by the user

rows.snackbar: Background color, text color, action color customizable
rows.toast: Limited — custom layout possible but complex

rows.snackbar: Preferred — richer UX, Material design compliant
rows.toast: Use only for simple, non-actionable messages

sections.heading: MaterialCardView Key Attributes
items: app:cardCornerRadius – Rounds the corners of the card (e.g., 12dp)
app:cardElevation – Sets the shadow depth below the card
app:strokeColor and app:strokeWidth – Adds a border/outline to the card
app:cardBackgroundColor – Sets the card's background color
android:checkable="true" – Makes the card checkable (toggles a checked overlay on click)
app:checkedIconDrawable – Icon shown on the card when it is in checked state
Wrap content in MaterialCardView instead of plain CardView for Material theming support
Use MaterialCardView as item containers in RecyclerView for consistent list item styling

rows.effect: Toolbar scrolls off screen as the user scrolls down
Toolbar reappears immediately when the user scrolls up
Toolbar only fully expands when scrolled to the top
Toolbar snaps to fully shown or fully hidden — no partial states
Toolbar collapses to its minimum height when scrolling down — used with CollapsingToolbarLayout

sections.heading: Material Design Color System
items: Primary — the main brand color; used for key components like the app bar, FAB, and filled buttons
Secondary — accent color for less prominent interactive elements like checkboxes and progress indicators
Tertiary — optional third color for additional contrast and visual interest
Surface — background color for components like cards, sheets, and dialogs
Background — the page/screen background color behind all content
Error — used consistently for all error states in input fields, snackbars, and icons
On* colors (onPrimary, onSurface, etc.) — colors for content drawn on top of the corresponding color
In Material 3, use the Material Theme Builder tool at m3.material.io to generate a full color scheme

rows.use_case: Hero text, large splash screen headings
Toolbar title, card titles, dialog titles
Main body text, descriptions, helper text
Button labels, chip text, tab labels, captions

sections.heading: Shape System in Material Design
items: Material Design defines shape categories: Extra Small, Small, Medium, Large, Extra Large, Full
Each shape category has a default corner style (rounded or cut) and corner size
Components are assigned a default shape category: Chips use Extra Small, Cards use Medium, Dialogs use Extra Large
Override shape globally in your theme: <item name="shapeAppearanceMediumComponent">@style/MyShape</item>
Override shape per component using app:shapeAppearanceOverlay in XML
Corner styles: RoundedCornerTreatment (circular) or CutCornerTreatment (45° cut)
Material 3 uses a more expressive shape scale with fully rounded components by default
Use ShapeableImageView for images with Material shape clipping (rounded, cut, diamond)

rows.reason: Ensures all components receive correct Material styling automatically
Enables floating labels, error messages, helper text, and icons
Applies Material theme to dialog automatically

rows.practice: Use Snackbar instead of Toast for user feedback
rows.reason: More accessible, supports undo action, follows Material guidelines

rows.practice: Define all colors in theme attributes, not hardcoded
rows.reason: Enables dynamic color, dark mode support, and easy rebranding
Automatic Fragment switching and back stack management with Navigation Component
Material themes provide dark mode variants — verify all components look correct

sections.heading: Summary
sections.content: Material Design Components provide a rich, polished, and accessible UI foundation for Android apps. By extending a Material theme and using MDC widgets — MaterialButton, TextInputLayout, MaterialCardView, BottomNavigationView, Snackbar, and more — you get a professional look and feel with minimal effort. Invest time in understanding the color system, typography scale, and shape system, as these theme attributes cascade through your entire app and give it a cohesive, branded identity that adapts to dark mode and Material You dynamic color.
